growth_records.strain: multiple isolates (includes S26, S96, S97 among others; exact n and per-strain % not given in main text)

up.method: calculated: species range 20-54% of 0.05 g/100mL urea (=8.325 mmol/L) hydrolyzed over 24 h; rate=(fraction x 8.325 mmol/L)/24h

growth_records.cite: Liu et al. Functional gene-guided enrichment plus in situ microsphere cultivation enables isolation of new crucial ureolytic bacteria from the rumen of cattle. Microbiome (2023). DOI:10.1186/s40168-023-01510-4. [PMC10105427]
growth_records.snippet: Strains of K. pneumoniae, C. koseri, C. amalonaticus, and A. butzleri hydrolyzed 20-54%, 11-42%, 2-27%, and 13-28%, respectively, of the urea added to the medium.
growth_records.notes: Range reflects variation among multiple K. pneumoniae strains isolated in this study (per-strain breakdown only in Fig. 6B, not in main text). Rate=(%hydrolyzed x 8.325 mmol/L urea)/24h, same assay conditions as S99 record. Volumetric, not gDW-normalized.

growth_records.medium: Anaerobic minimal medium with dextran (CSTR feed)
growth_records.cite: Khazaei et al. Metabolic multistability and hysteresis in a model aerobe-anaerobe microbiome community. Science Advances (2020). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aba0353. [PMC7423363]
growth_records.snippet: Temperature was maintained at 37°C, and a residence time of 5 hours (input and output flow rates of 40 ml/hour) was used for all experiments... For every experimental condition examined (input glucose concentration) in the CSTR, we waited f
growth_records.notes: CSTR steady state; dilution rate D = Fin/V = 40 ml/h / 200 ml = 0.2 h-1 (residence time 5 h). At chemostat steady state mu = D = 0.2 h-1 for the persisting species. Kp persists at steady state in all 8 glucose conditions (0.25-5 mM), so mu_Kp = 0.2 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.2 = 3.47 h. Medium salt-mix components (NaCl 17.6, CaCl2 0.4, MgCl2.6H2O 0.4, MnCl2.4H2O 0.2, CoCl2.6H2O 0.2 g/L) given as 20x stock used at 1x, so final = stock/20 (e.g. NaCl 17.6/20=0.88 g/L). Strain MGH-78578 is the genome-scale model strain (iYL1228); experimental K. pneumoniae strain not separately named. No experimental qS/qP tabulated in text (uptake/secretion are FBA-derived intracellular fluxes; model vmax=10 mmol/gCDW/h, Km=0.01 mM are E. coli literature parameters, not measured).

up.units: vmax fitted (Monod), value in table S1
up.method: batch OD600 kinetics, Bayesian MCMC fit

growth_records.cite: Khazaei et al. Metabolic multistability and hysteresis in a model aerobe-anaerobe microbiome community. Science Advances (2020). DOI:10.1126/sciadv.aba0353. [PMC7423363]
growth_records.snippet: Overnight cultures grown anaerobically in minimal medium supplemented with... 0.5% (w/v) glucose... resuspended... in carbon-source-free minimal medium... various concentrations [0.125 to 0.5% (w/v)] of the carbon source. The plate was incu
growth_records.notes: Batch mu_max and Monod vmax/Km for Kp on glucose were fitted (Monod: dX/dt=X*mu_max*S/(S+K)) but the numeric parameter values are reported only in table S1 / fig. S3 (supplementary), not in the article fulltext, so growth_rate and uptake rate left null. No arithmetic possible from in-text numbers.
